Fan-shaped green seaweeds on Singapore shores: how to tell them apart

green seaweeds text index | photo index Seaweeds

Division Chlorophyta Fan-shaped green seaweeds compared
How to tell them apart?
updated Jan 13 These fan-shaped seaweeds are sometimes confused for one another. Here’s more on how to tell them apart.

****

****

Solitary fan green seaweed
Avrainvillea erecta

Solitary fan green seaweed
Avrainvillea erecta

Ruffled fan green seaweed
Udotea sp.

One paddle-shaped blade flat not ruffled, thick rather stiff, sometimes divided into four or more ‘wings’.

Paddle-shaped blade flat usually with ruffled margins, thick rather stiff, usually in clusters of a few blades.

Clustered fan green seaweed
Avrainvillea sp.

Clustered fan green seaweed
Avrainvillea sp.

Pleated fan green seaweed Rhipidosiphon javensis

Circular blade flat not ruffled, thick rather stiff, usually in clusters of many blades.

Fan-shaped blade flat, thin and rather floppy when out of water, edges not ruffled although the blade amy be folded, usually in clusters of many blades.
